channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Syrian insurgents say they have entered the city of Hama after fierce fighting
item.description: Syrian insurgents say they have entered the central city of Hama after three days of intense clashes with government forces on its outskirts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alleged Chinese cyberattack: FBI urges encrypted apps
item.description: Salt Typhoon, a Chinese government-linked hacking group, is one of the largest intelligence breaches in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: UK leader Starmer will outline his 'Plan for Change.' Just don't call it a relaunch
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is trying to change the narrative on his five-month-old government.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What's next: South Korean leader's martial law declaration threatens his political career
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning martial law declaration lasted just hours, but experts say it raised serious questions about his ability to govern for the remaining 2 1/2 years of his term and whether he will abide by democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president's martial law declaration a 'blunder,' could embolden North Korea, expert says
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law and now faces impeachment over the surprising move from opposition lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 hours of anger, bravery and defiance during martial law in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol shocked the nation when he declared martial law and called in troops to secure the National Assembly building where parliament sits.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel is committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects the allegations
item.description: Amnesty International accuses Israel of committing genocide in the Gaza Strip during its war with Hamas, saying it has sought to deliberately destroy Palestinians by mounting deadly attacks, demolishing vital infrastructure and preventing the delivery of humanitarian aid.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: French government collapses in no-confidence vote
item.description: French lawmakers ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier with a no-confidence vote in the government Wednesday, the first time since 1962.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes on a Gaza tent camp kill at least 21 people, hospital says
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes tore through a tent camp for displaced Palestinians in southern Gaza on Wednesday, sparking fires and killing at least 21 people, according to the head of a nearby hospital, in the latest assault on a sprawling tent city that Israel designated a huma
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers see 'bad guys on both sides' in Syria offensive
item.description: Senators on both sides of the aisle are holding back from cheering rebel gains against Syrian President Bashar Assad, following a lightning offensive over the weekend led by Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), a U.S.-designated terrorist group.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Iranian activist, Nobel prize winner Mohammadi released from jail for medical treatment
item.description: Human rights activist Narges Mohammadi, who is imprisoned in Iran on charges including spreading propaganda, had her jail term suspended to undergo medical treatment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson rejects Biden request for $24 billion on new Ukraine aid
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson on Wednesday rejected a White House request to pass $24 billion in additional aid for Ukraine by the end of the year, saying any further assistance for Kyiv would be decided by President-elect Trump when he assumes office in January.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hispanic senators press Biden to protect immigrants before leaving White House
item.description: The Senate’s three current Hispanic Democrats are calling on President Biden to extend and strengthen humanitarian protections for certain groups of immigrants whose legal status could be stripped by the incoming Trump administration. In a letter on Wednesday, Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto (Nev.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Opposition party leader arrested as Georgia authorities try to curb protests over ending EU talks
item.description: Georgian police have raided the offices of an opposition party and arrested its leader in an apparent attempt to squelch a wave of mass protests over the decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Sophiko Megrelidze, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU condemns reported Taliban move to suspend medical education for women and girls
item.description: The European Union on Wednesday condemned the Taliban for violating human rights and women's access to education after media reports that the Taliban's leader has ordered private and public institutions to stop providing medical courses for women and girls in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Archaeologists in Denmark discover over 100 weapons from the Iron Age during excavations
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark unearthed what they've suggested is a sacrifice of weapons. The weapons likely belonged to a powerful chieftain thousands of years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippines says China's coast guard fired water cannons and sideswiped its patrol vessel
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Chinese coast guard vessels backed by navy ships fired powerful water cannons and blocked and sideswiped a Philippine patrol vessel on Wednesday in renewed aggression at a disputed shoal in the South China Sea, Philippine officials said.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine ceasefire terms come into focus as Trump returns
item.description: Potential paths to a ceasefire in Ukraine are coming into focus as President-elect Trump approaches Inauguration Day, with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky signaling his concessions to end the war largely along the current front lines in the east.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Hamas and Fatah are near an agreement on who will oversee postwar Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinian officials say Fatah and Hamas are closing in on an agreement to appoint a committee of politically independent technocrats to administer the Gaza Strip after the war. It would effectively end Hamas' rule and could help advance ceasefire talks with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Israeli strike kills a person in Lebanon, further shaking the tenuous ceasefire
item.description: NABATIYEH, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces carried out several new drone and artillery strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday, including a deadly strike that the Health Ministry and state media said killed one person, further shaking a tenuous ceasefire meant to end more than a year of fighting with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power shortages in Ecuador are melting away the future of a small town’s ice-cream industry
item.description: SALCEDO, Ecuador (AP) — Ice-cream production in Salcedo, a quaint town in Ecuador's central highlands, began in the mid-20th century, born from the ingenuity of Franciscan nuns.
item.dc:creator: GONZALO SOLANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Floods wreak havoc in Malaysia, southern Thailand with over 30 killed, tens of thousands displaced
item.description: KOTA BARU, Malaysia (AP) — Severe floods caused by monsoon rains killed more than 30 people and displaced tens of thousands in Malaysia and southern Thailand, officials said Tuesday, with both countries preparing shelters and evacuation plans in anticipation of more heavy rain.
item.dc:creator: SYAWALLUDIN ZAIN and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu reacts after Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't free hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says President-elect Trump has the emphasis in "the right place" with his latest demand for Hamas to release hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO chief wants less talk of peace process in Ukraine, more arms
item.description: NATO’s Secretary-General Mark Rutte argued that Ukraine needs to be put in a “position of strength” instead of being pushed toward peace talks, while speaking at a Tuesday conference ahead of the organization’s Foreign Ministers meeting this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: South Korean parliament blocks president's martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol attempted to declare martial law in the country for the first time in 45 years but was blocked by a parliament vote early Wednesday morning local time. He vowed to lift the order after hours of chaos and protests in the capital of Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Chicago welcomes eight children from Gaza for medical treatment
item.description: Over the next few months, the children will receive medical treatment at U.S. hospitals, free of cost.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Israel warns it will go after Lebanon directly if cease-fire with Hezbollah collapses
item.description: Israel issued a warning at Lebanon that it will not long distinguish between Beirut and Hezbollah should the cease-fire between Jerusalem and the terrorist group collapse following an exchange of strikes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president declares martial law in move against opposition party
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law on Tuesday and accused the opposition party of taking the parliamentary process hostage with "anti-state" activities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF soldiers accuse UN peacekeepers of enabling Hezbollah terrorists amid increasing cease-fire violations
item.description: In southern Lebanon, Israeli forces confront entrenched Hezbollah positions and question UNIFIL’s ability to enforce its mandate even before the cease-fire agreement was drawn up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American-Israeli IDF platoon commander killed in battle, body held in Gaza, IDF says
item.description: A 21-year-old American-Israeli believed to have been held hostage since October 2023 was confirmed dead by the Israel Defense Forces on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stowaway who boarded New York-to-Paris flight will be sent back to US
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Authorities are investigating after a Russian national who did not have a ticket boarded a Paris-bound Delta flight at New York's John F. Kennedy International Airport this week and was arrested when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation
